architecture.summary: LaPael applies input-dependent noise to early layers of large language models during fine-tuning to generate diverse, semantically consistent paraphrases of training data in latent space. The noised activations flow through the model's remaining layers, creating augmented training examples without external paraphrase generation. Information crosses the model boundary as modified hidden-state activations.
trained_components: downstream layers fine-tuned on noised activations

biblio.title: Latent Paraphrasing: Perturbation on Layers Improves Knowledge Injection in Language Models

baseline.name: standard fine-tuning and existing noise-based approaches

claims.statement: LaPael improves knowledge injection over standard fine-tuning and existing noise-based approaches
Latent-level paraphrasing method applies input-dependent noise to early LLM layers to enable diverse and semantically consistent augmentations directly within the model
LaPael eliminates the recurring costs of paraphrase generation for each knowledge update
Combining LaPael with data-level paraphrasing further enhances performance
